---
title: "Immigration GLOA 101"
author: "Byunghwan Son <br> George Mason University"
date: " "
#########################################
linkcolor: red
citecolor: blue
urlcolor: blueß
output: 
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    seal: false
    nature:
      ratio: '16:9'
      highlightStyle: github
      mathjax: "default"  
  #    self-contained: TRUE
  chakra: css/libs/remark-latest.min.js
css: ["default", 
      "css/footer.css", 
      "css/color.css", 
      # "css/xaringan-themer.css",
      default-fonts]
lib_dir: css/libs
beforeInit: "css/macros.js"
# seal: false  
#bibliography: /Users/bson3/Dropbox/latex/mybib2.bib
#remotes::install_github('yihui/xaringan', upgrade = TRUE)

---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# to use FontAwesome
#htmltools::tagList(rmarkdown::html_dependency_font_awesome())
library(fs)
library(fontawesome)
# library(magick)  
library(ggplot2)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, error=F, echo=F)
options(htmltools.preserve.raw = FALSE)
```

class: inverse, center, top
background-image: url(https://news.miami.edu/_assets/images-stories/2020/05/migrationsymp_hero_940x529.jpg)
background-size: contain

# .huge[**Global Immigration**, GLOA 101] <br> <br>


```{r results="asis", fig.width=16, fig.height=7, cache=T}


library(refugees)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)




```

---

.full-width-blue[

# .Large[Things to talk about:]

]
<br>
--

# 1. confusing terminologies and concepts

--

# 2. immigrants (from and to)

--

# 3. refugeees (from and to)

--

# 4. why so much hate?

---


class: inverse
background-image: url(https://github.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/blob/master/images/background_leftEmpty3.jpeg?raw=true)
background-size: 100%

# .Large[Terminologies]

--

# - .large[.yellow[immigrants]]

# - .large[.orange[refugees]]

# - .large[.Green[asylum-seekers]]

# - .large[.pink[migrants]]

---


class: inverse
background-image: url(https://github.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/blob/master/images/background_leftEmpty3.jpeg?raw=true)
background-size: 100%

# .Large[Terminologies]

## - .large[.yellow[immigrants]]: legal, documented, permanent

## - .large[.orange[refugees]]: legal, documented, semi-permanent, humanitarian cause

## - .large[.Green[asylum-seekers]]: undocumented, temporary

## - .large[.pink[migrants]: broader



---


# Immigration 'stock' (% population) trend

--

```{r message=FALSE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results="asis", fig.width=16, fig.height=7, cache=T}

library(ggrepel)

wdi %>% 
  drop_na(GDPpc) %>%
  drop_na(m.stock.per) %>%
  mutate(developed = 
           case_when(GDPpc > 10000 ~ "developed",
                     GDPpc < 10000 ~ "underdeveloped"
                     )) %>%
  mutate(large = 
           case_when(m.stock.per > 70 & developed=="developed" ~ country,
                     m.stock.per > 30 & developed=="underdeveloped" ~ country)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y=m.stock.per, 
             x=year,
             color=developed)) +
  geom_label_repel(aes(label=large, color=developed), size=7) +
  geom_smooth(se=FALSE, linetype="dashed") +
  geom_jitter(alpha=0.1, 
              width = 1,
              size=8) +
  labs(y="Migrant Stock % of population",
       x="Year") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size=0),
        legend.text = element_text(size=25),
        axis.text=element_text(size=25),
        axis.title=element_text(size=25))

```     
---

# Refugees by .orange[Origins] .tiny[(UNHCR data including internally displaced ppl)]

--

.pull-left[

```{r}

refugees::population |>
  filter(year == 1990) |>
  summarise(refugees = sum(refugees, na.rm = TRUE) + 
              sum(oip, na.rm = TRUE),
            .by = coo_name) |>
  slice_max(order_by = refugees, n = 10) |>
  ggplot(aes(refugees, reorder(coo_name, refugees))) +
  geom_col(fill = 'blue',
           width = 0.8,
           alpha=0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label_comma()(refugees)),
            hjust = -0.2) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
  labs(y="",
       title="Year: 1980") +
  xlim(0, 7000000)

ggsave(filename = 'ref1980.png')
```

]

--

.pull-right[

```{r}

refugees::population |>
    filter(year == 2020) |>
    summarise(refugees = sum(refugees, na.rm = TRUE) + 
                sum(oip, na.rm = TRUE),
              .by = coo_name) |>
    slice_max(order_by = refugees, n = 10) |>
    ggplot(aes(refugees, reorder(coo_name, refugees))) +
    geom_col(fill = 'red',
             width = 0.8,
             alpha=0.6) +
    geom_text(aes(label = label_comma()(refugees)),
              hjust = 0.5) +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
    labs(y="",
         title="Year: 2020") +
  xlim(0, 7000000)



ggsave(filename = 'ref2020.png')

```     

]


---

# Refugees by .green[Destinations] (UNHCR data)

--

.pull-left[

```{r}

  refugees::population |>
    filter(year == 1980) |>
    summarise(refugees = sum(refugees, na.rm = TRUE) + 
                sum(oip, na.rm = TRUE),
              .by = coa_name) |>
    slice_max(order_by = refugees, n = 10) |>
    ggplot(aes(refugees, reorder(coa_name, refugees))) +
    geom_col(fill = 'blue',
             width = 0.8,
             alpha=0.6) +
    geom_text(aes(label = label_comma()(refugees)),
              hjust = 0.5) +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
    labs(y="",
         title="Year: 1980") +
  xlim(0, 4000000)


```   

]

--

.pull-right[

```{r}


refugees::population |>
    filter(year == 2020) |>
    summarise(refugees = sum(refugees, na.rm = TRUE) + 
                sum(oip, na.rm = TRUE),
              .by = coa_name) |>
    slice_max(order_by = refugees, n = 10) |>
    ggplot(aes(refugees, reorder(coa_name, refugees))) +
    geom_col(fill = 'red',
             width = 0.8,
             alpha=0.6) +
    geom_text(aes(label = label_comma()(refugees)),
              hjust = 0.5) +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
    labs(y="",
         title="Year: 2020") +
  xlim(0, 4000000)


```

]

class: inverse, center, middle
background-color: black
background-image: url(https://c.tenor.com/Cw777VnGhWwAAAAC/slamming-door-shutting-door.gif)
background-position: right
background-size: contain

# .Large[What do .bluey[hosts] think?] 

--

## When do they want to shut the door? `r fa("door-open")`




---
class: inverse, right, bottom
background-image: url(https://www.stashmedia.tv/wp-content/uploads/Screenshot-2017-12-19-08.20.55-640x359.png)
background-size: contain

# What about .pink[Refugees]?

## When are hosts reluctant? <br> .Yellow[Contact Theory]?

--

# **Hangartner et al. (2019)**

---

class: right, bottom
background-image: url(https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/master/images/Hangartner2019.png)
background-size: contain
background-position: left

# What about .pink[Refugees]?

## When are hosts reluctant? <br> .Yellow[Contact Theory]?

# **Hangartner et al. (2019)**

---

# The rationale: scene 1 .tiny[(when locals are .green[familiar] with the arrivals)]

<iframe width="1200" height="500" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/-zMAICROFQ8?si=p3I3fAkqv_NMzzjh" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>


---

# The rationale: scene 2 .tiny[(when locals are .red[NOT] familiar with the arrivals)]

<iframe width="1200" height="500" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/OBXczZzZ1VU?si=cU5gznY8O7zQo0j2" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

---

class: right, bottom
background-image: url(https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/master/images/Hangartner2019a.png)
background-size: contain
background-position: left


## When are hosts reluctant? <br> .Yellow[Contact Theory]?

# **Hangartner et al. (2019)**

---

.full-width-green[
# What do we know:
]
<br>
--

## 1. (Im)Migration is nothing new, though the patterns have .orange[evolved] a bit.

--

## 2. Immigrats and refugees are .blue[different] in their behavioral patterns.

--

## 3. On the .purple[receiving] end, not so much.

--

## 4. More exposure tends to lead to more .red[reluctance].
